content: Zinc deficiency is one of the most common reasons for dry skin, especially when trying to build muscle mass.
Biotin deficiency can lead to dry skin due to the presence of avidin in egg whites, which binds to biotin and reduces its absorption.
Eating egg whites without the yolks can lead to biotin deficiency because the yolk contains biotin while the white contains avidin.
Essential fatty acid deficiencies can cause dry skin, particularly during periods of tissue expansion such as pregnancy or muscle growth.
Arachidonic acid, an omega-6 fatty acid, is crucial for preventing dry skin and is best obtained from liver and egg yolks.
Liver and egg yolks are the best sources of biotin and arachidonic acid, respectively, to help prevent dry skin.
